title: nestwork.config.json
description: Directory-level metadata declaring whether and how content from a working directory may be ingested into a Nestwork repository. Lives in the source working directory, not inside Nestwork. See AGENTS.md Section 9 and docs/workflow-protocol.md.

$schema.description: Optional JSON Schema reference for editor support.

ingest.description: Declares whether and where content from this directory may be ingested into Nestwork.

target.description: Which Nestwork category receives ingested content. null = not ingestable.

name.description: Destination filename or subfolder under the target category. Required when target is non-null.

desensitize.description: How content from this directory must be desensitized before ingestion.

level.description: Desensitization aggressiveness. 'none' = no transformation (only valid for verifiably non-confidential content). 'weak' = pattern-based redaction using custom_rules. 'strong' = AI-driven semantic desensitization following docs/desensitization-prompt.md plus all custom_rules.

custom_rules.description: User-defined sensitive terms or patterns specific to this directory (employer names, internal codenames, client names, etc.). Layered on top of the global methodology. Never committed to upstream nestwork — these stay in the user's source directory only.

placeholder_overrides.description: Optional mapping from sensitive term to preferred placeholder. Overrides the default placeholder vocabulary in docs/desensitization-prompt.md.
